---
title: "Import Data"
---

```{=html}
<script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function() {
  document.querySelectorAll(".foldable").forEach(function(block) {

    // hide initially
    block.style.display = "none";

    const toggle = document.createElement("button");
    toggle.textContent = "Show Output";

    block.parentNode.insertBefore(toggle, block);

    toggle.addEventListener("click", function() {
      const isHidden = block.style.display === "none";
      if (isHidden) {
        block.style.display = "block";
        toggle.textContent = "Hide Output";
      } else {
        block.style.display = "none";
        toggle.textContent = "Show Output";
      }
    });
  });
});
</script>
```

## Importing data in LISSY

LIS and LWS datasets are split by country, year, and level (household and individual). To load data in LISSY, we can use the following two methods.

### Method 1: `lissyuse`

Using the `lissyuse()` function from our in-house [lissyrtools](https://lis-cross-national-data-center.github.io/lissyrtools/) package has three main advantages:

1.  Imports all datasets available, given specific ISO2 country codes by default;

2.  Automatically selects the correct data level based on the variables requested;

3.  If both household-level and person-level variables are selected, the corresponding files are automatically merged for each country–year.

```{r, echo=TRUE , message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(lissyrtools)
```

#### Advantage 1

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, results = 'hold' }

#' We can load all the datasets available in LIS 
#'  for Mexico and the USA, simply by inputting their ISO2 codes.

lis_data <- lissyrtools::lissyuse(
  data = c("mx", "us"), 
  vars = "dhi",
)

# The output of `lissyrtools::lissyuse()` will always be a list of data frames. 
class(lis_data)
names(lis_data)
```



```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, results = 'hold' }

#' For a shorter time span, 
#'  the user can also define the `from` and `to` arguments.

lis_data <- lissyrtools::lissyuse(
  data = c("mx", "us"), 
  vars = "dhi",
  from = 2014,
  to = 2016
)
```

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, results = 'hold' }

# Specific datasets can also be selected by specifying its `ccyy` code

lis_data <- lissyrtools::lissyuse(
  data = c("it16", "mx", "us"), 
  vars = "dhi",
  from = 2014,
  to = 2016
)

```

#### Advantage 2

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, results = 'hold'}

#' Based on the variables defined in the `vars` argument, 
#'  `lissyuse()` will select the level of the datasets in accordance.  

h_level_data <- lissyrtools::lissyuse(
  data = "lu18", 
  vars = c("dhi", "region_c", "typehh") # only h-level 
)

# Names of the data frames in the list will have an `h` suffix.
```

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| echo: true
#| output: hold

p_level_data <- lissyrtools::lissyuse(
  data = "lu18", 
  vars = c("age", "sex", "relation", "emp", "pi11") # only p-level 
)

# Names of the data frames in the list will have a `p` suffix.
```

#### Advantage 3

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, results = 'hold'}

#' If both h-level and p-level variables are selected
#'  then `lissyuse()` will automatically merge both files in one single data frame. 

merged_hhd_ind_data <- lissyrtools::lissyuse(
  data = "lu18", 
  vars = c("dhi", "region_c", "typehh", "age", "sex", "relation", "emp", "pi11")
)

# Names of the data frames will not have a suffix.

#' NOTE: some variables are always imported by default, 
#'  with no need to be defined explicitly. 
names(merged_hhd_ind_data[[1]])
```

#### Importing LWS data

To import LWS data, one would need to change the selection in the `Project` drop-down menu, and add the `lws` a logical argument.

![](/images/project_lws.png){fig-align="center"}

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE}
us_lws_data = lissyrtools::lissyuse(
  data = c("us"), 
  vars = c("dnw"),  # disposable net wealth - LWS specific variable. 
  from = 2015, 
  lws = TRUE        # `lws` logical argument  
)

names(us_lws_data[[1]])
```

#### Auxilliary Functions

In `lissyrtools` there are other auxiliary functions concerning the selection of the correct ISO2 codes and the LIS / LWS variables.

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE,  results = 'hold', class.output = "foldable"}
lissyrtools::get_countries_lis()
lissyrtools::get_countries_lws()
```

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, class.output = "foldable"}
lissyrtools::get_years_lis(c("es", "de", "it", "fr", "cz", "pl")) 
```

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE,  results = 'hold', class.output = "foldable"}
# To check the labels of the variables imported
lissyrtools::variable_labels(vars = merged_hhd_ind_data) 
lissyrtools::variable_labels(vars = us_lws_data)
```

### Method 2: `read.LIS()`

Alternatively, we have a less automated version, used for longer, imports all the ccyy, by (level) separarteyl, and appends them in a single `data.frame` and not in a `list`.


```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, include=FALSE}
source("../src/read.LIS.R")
```

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# put here the code of read.LIS, then on a hidden file, such that is doesn't go to the github. 
source("../src/read.LIS.R") 
```

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, results = 'hold'}
data_h <- read.LIS(c("de18h", "es18h")) # Import h-level data for Lux. 2010 
data_p <- read.LIS(c("de18p", "es18p")) # Import p-level data for Lux. 2010 

full_data <- merge(data_p, data_h, by = c("dname", "hid"))  
class(full_data) 
```

#### Importing LWS data

To import LWS data, the syntax remains exactly the same, but again the Project must be changed in the drop-down menu.

![](/images/project_lws.png){fig-align="center"}


```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# put here the code of read.LIS, then on a hidden file, such that is doesn't go to the github. 
source("../src/read.LIS_for_lws.R") 
```

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, results = 'hold'}
varh <- c('hid', 'dname', 'own', 'dnw', 'inum')
varp <- c('hid', 'dname', 'inum', 'ppopwgt', 'age', 'sex', 'relation', 'emp', 'ptime1')


data_h <- read.LIS(c("de17h", "es17h"), vars = varh)
data_p <- read.LIS(c("de17p", "es17p"), vars = varp)

full_data <- merge(data_h, data_p, by = c("dname", "hid")) 
df <- merge(data_p , data_h , by = c("dname", "hid", "inum"))

names(df)
```

#### Importing LWS files on replicate weights

At the moment, this is the only method that allows users to import the files with replicate weights for LWS.

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE, results = 'hold', class.output = "foldable"}

varh <- c('hid', 'dname', 'own', 'dnw', 'inum')

data_h <- read.LIS("us19h", vars = varh)
file_r <- read.LIS("us19r")

df <- merge(data_h, file_r, by = "hid")
names(df)
```
